title: Pea & Ham Soup
keywords: Marco Pierre White inspired Thermomix recipe
intro: Marco Pierre White is all about big flavour from simple things. This pea and ham soup is comfort itself — the Thermomix cooks and blends it smooth in one bowl.
steps: Chop onion 4 sec / speed 5. Add butter and sauté 3 min / 120°C / speed 1.
Add the peas, half the ham, stock and mint. Cook 15 min / 100°C / speed 1.
Blend 1 min / speed 8 until smooth. Stir the rest of the ham through, season and finish with a swirl of cream.
tips: Keep a little ham back to stir through at the end for texture.
A pinch of mint keeps the peas tasting fresh and sweet.

faq.q: Can I use a ham hock?
faq.a: Yes — simmer a hock in the stock first for deeper flavour, then shred the meat in.

faq.q: Is this the original Marco Pierre White recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Marco Pierre White and pea & ham soup; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.

faq.q: Can I make this Pea & Ham Soup in a TM6 or TM7?
faq.a: Yes. The method is written with TM6 and TM7 settings, using metric weights, clear times, temperatures and speeds.

description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Marco Pierre White-style pea & ham soup: metric ingredients, exact TM6 and TM7 times, temperatures and speeds for Australian home cooks.
